title: Learning from label proportions for a visual matrix speller (ERP)
description: This dataset comprises event-related potential (ERP) recordings from 13 healthy subjects performing a visual P300 speller task using a 6×7 character matrix. The study introduces learning from label proportions (LLP), an unsupervised classification approach that enables calibration-free brain-computer interface operation by exploiting known target/non-target stimulus ratios. Subjects completed three copy-spelling sessions without prior calibration, achieving 84.5% character accuracy, demonstrating the feasibility of LLP for practical BCI applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 1000 Hz from 31 channels using a BrainAmp DC amplifier with passive Ag/AgCl electrodes in a standard 10-20 montage (nose reference, FCz ground). Subjects performed a visual copy-spelling task using a 6×7 grid with two stimulus sequences having different target/non-target ratios (3:8 and 2:18). Each character required 68 highlighting events. The LLP classifier exploited these known proportions to reconstruct mean target and non-target responses without labeled training data. Auxiliary channels recorded EOG, pulse, respiration, and EMG. Sessions lasted approximately 3 hours including setup.

funding_references.funder_name: Special Research Fund from Ghent University
